title: Heavy Menstrual Bleeding - Menorrhagia
chief_complaint: 35-year-old with heavy menstrual bleeding for 6 months, soaking pads every 2 hours, clots, fatigue. No intermenstrual bleeding.

physical_exam.pelvic: Normal size uterus, no adnexal masses

key_learning_points: Endometrial biopsy if >45 or risk factors

patient_responses.bleeding: I soak through a pad every 2 hours

feedback.correct_message: Well diagnosed! Menorrhagia with iron deficiency.
feedback.incorrect_message: This is menorrhagia. The microcytic anemia + low ferritin confirm it.
feedback.attending_note: Menorrhagia: Ferrous sulfate 325mg TID, tranexamic acid 1g TID during menses, OCPs or LNG-IUS. Endometrial biopsy if >45 or refractory. Good response expected.
